name: Ernmas
description: Ernmas, a significant figure in Irish mythology, is often described as a mother goddess or a powerful sorceress. She is primarily known as the mother of several important deities and figures, including the goddesses Ériu, Banba, Fódla (the three tutelary goddesses of Ireland), and sometimes also Macha, Mórrígan, and others depending on the source. This association with multiple powerful goddesses highlights her role as a progenitor of strength, sovereignty, and magical prowess. Ernmas is not typically associated with specific stories or exploits of her own, but her influence is felt through her descendants. Her death, often described as occurring during a battle or plague, is considered a pivotal event that marked a shift in the cosmic order. Some accounts also link her to agricultural fertility, suggesting a broader role in the prosperity of the land. She embodies the ancient, powerful feminine force that shapes the destiny of Ireland and its people. Her legacy is carried on by her daughters, who continue to represent aspects of the land, war, and sovereignty. Ernmas's role, though often behind the scenes, is crucial to understanding the complex web of relationships and power dynamics within the Irish mythological pantheon. She represents the source from which many of the most important female figures of Irish myth draw their power and authority. She may have been a goddess of agriculture and fertility, and her death is said to have brought about a great famine.
subtitle: Mother of Sovereignty and Power

keyMyths.title: The Birth of the Goddesses
keyMyths.description: Ernmas is the mother of Ériu, Banba, and Fódla, the three goddesses who represent Ireland itself. Their birth signifies the divine connection to the land and its sovereignty.

keyMyths.title: The Death of Ernmas
keyMyths.description: Ernmas died during a plague or battle, bringing misfortune to the land. Her death is a symbolic representation of the loss of a powerful, maternal figure.

keyMyths.title: Ernmas and Fertility
keyMyths.description: Some accounts suggest Ernmas was connected to agricultural fertility. The health of the land and its ability to provide for the people was closely linked to her.

sources.author: Patricia Monaghan
